title: I1B deterministic resource transitions and harness integrity evaluation
acceptance_criteria: resource-transition v1 documentation defines deterministic electrical and thermal order, safe arithmetic, outcome semantics, non-goals, and scientific limitations
transition engine returns completed and constraint_violation as domain outcomes with process exit 0 for valid scenarios
malformed input, invalid scenario contracts, missing files, unsupported options, and arithmetic overflow return nonzero process exits
canonical .\eng.ps1 verify covers nominal, constraint, invalid-input, deterministic-output, capability, and boundary-preserving checks without dependencies, providers, adapters, or network calls
baseline, red, green, redaction, tamper, reproducibility, stale-binding, and final evidence are generated under ignored artifacts and bound or classified honestly
review packet and reviewer output bind to the reviewed source commit, with stale artifacts rejected or identified
candidate lifecycle transitions remain explicit; no candidate is verified automatically and no canonical KB write occurs

status metadata reports only resource scenario contract, validation, and deterministic transition as implemented while scheduler, workloads, providers, adapters, and operational capabilities remain false
